content:
# 2 Corinthians Chapter 13 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2CO_013_page_001.jpg

1. This third [time] I am coming to you. "By [the] mouth of two witnesses and three every word will be established."

2. I have said beforehand, and I say beforehand, as being present the second [time] and being absent now, to the [ones] having previously sinned and to all the rest, that if I come again I will not spare,

3. since you seek proof of the Christos speaking in me — who toward you is not weak but is powerful among you.

4. For indeed he was crucified from weakness, but he lives from [the] power of God. For indeed we are weak in him, but we will live with him from [the] power of God toward you.

5. Examine yourselves [to see] whether you are in the faith. Test yourselves. Or do you not recognize about yourselves that Yeshua Christos is in you? — unless you are unapproved.

6. And I hope that you will know that we are not unapproved.

7. And we pray to God that you do nothing evil — not that we may appear approved, but that you may do the good, and we may be as unapproved.

8. For we are not able [to do] anything against the truth, but for the truth.

9. For we rejoice when we are weak and you are powerful. And this also we pray for: the restoration of you.

10. Because of this I write these [things] being absent, that being present I may not deal sharply, according to the authority which the Lord gave to me for building up and not for tearing down.

11. Finally, brothers, rejoice, be restored, be encouraged, be of one mind, be at peace, and the God of the love and of peace will be with you.

12. Greet one another with a holy kiss.

13. All the holy [ones] greet you.

14. The grace of the Lord Yeshua Christos and the love of God and the fellowship of the Holy Spirit [be] with all of you.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 — Deuteronomy 19:15:** "By the mouth of two or three witnesses" — Paul applies the legal requirement for testimony to his third visit. Whether the "witnesses" are his three visits or actual witnesses is debated.

2. **v.4 — "crucified from weakness":** ἐσταυρώθη ἐξ ἀσθενείας — the crucifixion was an act of weakness (Christ submitted to death). But from that weakness comes resurrection power. This is the christological foundation for Paul's entire theology of weakness in the letter.

3. **v.5 — "Examine yourselves":** ἑαυτοὺς πειράζετε — Paul turns the test back on the Corinthians. They demanded proof of Christ speaking in Paul (v.3); Paul demands they test themselves.

4. **v.9 — "restoration":** κατάρτισις — "mending/fitting together/restoration." The only use of this noun in the NT. The cognate verb (καταρτίζω) means "to mend" (cf. mending nets, Matt 4:21).

5. **v.14 — The trinitarian benediction:** The most explicitly triadic formula in Paul. Grace (Christ), love (God/Father), fellowship (Holy Spirit). This became the basis for liturgical benedictions in Christian worship.

— EMET, AI Translator
